Salesforce FY2026 record revenue of $41.5B (+10% YoY); FCF $14.4B. New $50B buyback authorized; quarterly dividend raised 5.8%. Informatica acquisition fully integrated.
No material news.

Broadcom stock trading at all-time highs driven by AI chip demand and VMware integration progress. No material credit-adverse events in last 60 days.
HP Inc Q2 FY2026 earnings due May 28; CEO Enrique Lores departed, Interim CEO Broussard in place. Q1 FY2026 revenue $14.4B (+6.9%); rising memory costs flagged, results guided to low end of range.
HPE Q2 FY2026 earnings due Jun 1; stock up 62% since Q1 results reported Mar 9. AI server demand strong; Juniper integration ongoing.
Dell FY2026 record revenue $113.5B (+19% YoY); record FCF $11.2B. FY27 revenue growth guided at 23% midpoint. Dividend raised 20%; $10B buyback authorization added.

Announced spin-off of data center EMS business (~$6.6Bn revenue) and acquisition of Electrical Power Products. S&P affirmed BBB-/Stable May 6 2026; pro forma leverage expected to remain below 3x.
Jabil Q3 FY2026 revenue up 18.7% YoY, beating estimates by 3.8%. AI infrastructure and cloud server demand continues to drive strong growth.
Arrow Electronics facing ongoing demand softness in components distribution; strategic review of IT solutions segment underway. Macro headwinds weigh on near-term revenue visibility.
Avnet Q3 FY2026 earnings due; stock up ~4.9% prior month. Memory supply constraints driving rising ASPs. Component distribution peers showing mixed demand signals.
TD Synnex Q1 FY2026 record non-GAAP gross billings and diluted EPS; revenue $17.2B (+18% YoY). Cloud and AI infrastructure driving outperformance across all operating regions.
Ingram Micro Q1 2026 net sales $14.0B (+13.7% YoY); guided Q2 net sales $13.6-14.0B. GPU and AI infrastructure projects driving growth; Middle East volatility flagged as minor risk.

Digital Realty FY2025 revenue $6.1B; guided 2026 revenue $6.6-6.7B with Adjusted EBITDA $3.6-3.7B. Record leasing backlog. BofA downgraded stock (not credit) citing AI capacity going to private operators.
